channel.title: Projects on Adam Conkey
channel.description: Recent content in Projects on Adam Conkey

item.title: Robot Control in Gazebo
item.description: Robot control framework for simulations in Gazebo. It is designed to be extensible, allowing easy addition of new controllers, and it works in conjunction with my robot interface package to decouple the controllers from the particular robot you’re using.

item.title: Real-Time Robot Control
item.description: Real-time Orocos controllers for the KUKA LBR4+ robot. Includes a controller switching framework for safely swapping controllers at runtime, and a simulated FRI (Fast Research Interface) component that allows Orocos components to be tested Gazebo.

item.title: Teleoperation Mediated by Virtual Environment
item.description: A kinematic simulator with force rendering created with rViz to provide a training ground to learn robot policies from demonstration using a Phantom Omni haptic input device. The learned policy can then be executed on a real robot (or a simulated robot in Gazebo).

item.title: rViz Trajectory Visualization
item.description: Package for generating trajectory visualizations in rViz, including dynamic real-time visualizations showing a customizable trace of the robot’s end-effector.

item.title: Baxter Demonstration Recorder
item.description: A demonstration recorder for the Baxter robot utilizing the button and display interfaces to make it easier to record demonstrations when operating Baxter in gravity compensation mode.

item.title: Robot Interface
item.description: Abstraction layer for controlling robots in a unified manner while accounting for robot platform-specific needs.

item.title: Data Logging
item.description: Two data logging frameworks for recording robot and sensor data. One uses HDF5 files with an h5py interface, and the other post-processes rosbag data into Pandas dataframes.

item.title: Baxter Multimaster
item.description: A fork of the multimaster package which I augmented to allow teleoperation of a slave Baxter robot by moving the limbs of master Baxter robot in gravity compensation mode.
